title: korthalsella remyana var. wawrae
intro: Korthalsella remyana var. wawrae, also known as Wawra's hook, is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of its host plants, typically occurring in tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, containing compounds that have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
